tldr_weapon: Sacrificial Fragments R5 (BiS) / Thrilling Tales R5 (support)
intro: Sucrose is a 4-star Anemo Catalyst support who provides Elemental Mastery sharing, enemy grouping, and Viridescent Venerer RES shred. Her A4 passive shares 20% of her EM with the team, and her C6 provides 20% Elemental DMG bonus. She's the F2P-friendly alternative to Kazuha, and in reaction-heavy teams (Taser, Vaporize, Aggravate), her EM sharing can actually outperform him. Sucrose also serves as the premier on-field driver for Taser teams, swirling Electro-Charged with every normal attack.
weapon_bottom_line: Sacrificial Fragments R5 is her best — EM substat + skill reset for double E = double particles + double grouping. Thrilling Tales R5 for pure support: 48% ATK buff for the next character.

weapons.name: Sacrificial Fragments R5
weapons.notes: Best overall. Highest EM among 4-star catalysts + skill reset enables double E for massive swirl damage.

weapons.name: A Thousand Floating Dreams
weapons.notes: Highest EM catalyst. Team EM/DMG buff based on elements. Overkill but BiS for pure EM.

weapons.name: Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers R5
weapons.notes: Pure support. 48% ATK buff for next character. No EM but the ATK buff can be more valuable than personal swirl damage.

weapons.name: Magic Guide R5
weapons.notes: Budget EM option. Higher EM than most 4-stars. Good if you don't have Sacrificial Fragments.

artifact_why: Mandatory for all Anemo supports. 60% Swirl DMG + 40% Elemental RES shred. Triples Sucrose's team contribution.
artifact_alt: None — 4pc VV is non-negotiable. Even with bad substats, the 4pc effect is more valuable than any other set.

main_stats.sands_note: Always EM. More EM = more team EM share + more swirl damage.
main_stats.goblet_note: Always EM. Anemo DMG goblet adds little — Sucrose's damage is from swirls, not Anemo.
main_stats.circlet_note: Always EM. Triple EM is the only build for support Sucrose.

sub_priority: 1. Elemental Mastery — as high as possible, target 800+
3. Flat ATK / ATK% — minor, only for personal Anemo damage
4. CRIT — irrelevant for full EM build

teams.name: Taser Driver
teams.desc: Classic Taser. Sucrose drives on-field, swirling Electro-Charged with every normal attack. Her EM share buffs the entire team's reaction damage.

teams.name: Vaporize Support
teams.desc: Sucrose provides VV shred + EM share for the Pyro DPS. Her grouping helps AoE Vaporize hits. Thrilling Tales buff further amplifies damage.

teams.name: Aggravate Support
teams.desc: Sucrose provides VV shred + EM share for the Electro DPS. Her grouping helps AoE Aggravate. Kazuha gives DMG%, Sucrose gives EM — pick based on team needs.

rotation_code: Fischl E (summon Oz) → Beidou E+Q (snapshot) → Xingqiu E+Q (rain swords) → Sucrose E+Q → Sucrose NA spam (swirl driver) → Sucrose E (refresh) → Repeat
rotation_key: Sucrose's normal attacks trigger Raincutter + Stormbreaker + Oz simultaneously. Use E whenever available for grouping and energy. Her Q infuses an element — prioritize Electro or Hydro for Taser.
mistakes: Building ATK/Crit on support Sucrose — her damage comes from swirls, which only scale with EM and level. ATK/Crit do almost nothing.
Not leveling to 90 — Swirl damage scales with character level. Level 80→90 is a ~34% swirl damage increase.
Using the wrong weapon — Sacrificial Fragments gives EM + double skill. Don't use Mappa Mare or other ATK% catalysts.
Not using VV set — without 4pc VV, Sucrose loses 40% RES shred. Even bad VV artifacts are better than perfect non-VV ones.

seo.title: Sucrose Build Guide — Best Weapons, Artifacts, Teams | GenshinGuide
seo.description: Complete Sucrose build guide for Genshin Impact v5.6. Best EM build, Viridescent Venerer artifacts, Taser driver and support team compositions, rotation guide.
